channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: India's Informal Sector: Debt Shrinks, Investment Falls
item.description: India's informal businesses are cutting debt, with loans dropping 20% and interest payments down 16%, according to the 2025 Annual Survey of Unincorporated Sector Enterprises. However, this debt reduction comes as investment in new fixed assets fell 14%, suggesting businesses are hesitant to expand. This trend, alongside uneven state performance and contrasting formal credit growth for MSMEs, points to cautious economic conditions and deeper issues.

item.title: US-China Summit: Trade Hopes Tested by Tech Barriers
item.description: The upcoming US-China summit on May 14-15 is a key event for global markets. While expectations for a major breakthrough are low, any easing of trade tensions could boost Chinese stocks. Investors are watching for signs that reduce geopolitical risk and address deep-seated disagreements. The talks will cover trade tariffs, technology export controls, rare earth supply chains, and farm purchases. Despite hopes for a calmer trade relationship, existing tariffs, tech restrictions, and competition for AI leadership present significant challenges that could stir market volatility.

item.title: Indian IT's AI Services Boom vs. US Infrastructure Valuation Surge
item.description: US tech giants pour billions into AI infrastructure, fueling high valuations. Indian IT firms excel at AI services, earning billions, yet face a valuation discount. This report explores the reasons behind this gap, looking at differing roles in AI development and implementation.

item.title: 8 Indian Stocks Earn Brokerage Buy Ratings Amid Market Caution
item.description: Indian brokerages are backing eight key stocks with 'Buy' ratings, highlighting strong performance, market gains, and clear earnings. These picks cover sectors from auto to IT and digital payments, with analysts predicting substantial gains. Still, investors need to watch competition, margins, and sector hurdles to see if the optimism holds.

item.title: India's Fuel Price Freeze: Billions Lost, Rupee Under Pressure
item.description: Despite a 36% surge in crude oil prices due to the West Asia conflict, India has maintained static petrol prices. This intervention, while politically popular, creates significant economic challenges. State-owned refineries face billions in monthly losses, the current account deficit is projected to widen, and the rupee faces sustained downward pressure. The strategy highlights India's vulnerability as a major energy importer, unlike nations with domestic production.

item.title: Indian Investors Pour ₹2.5 Lakh Crore Into Stocks, Backing Domestic Growth
item.description: In the January-March 2026 quarter, Indian institutional investors (DIIs) injected ₹2.5 lakh crore into Indian equities, showing strong confidence despite market turbulence. Mutual funds were key drivers, offsetting selling by foreign investors (FIIs) and retail traders. DIIs increased stakes in companies with strong domestic demand, digital capabilities, and expanding operations, focusing on housing finance, fintech, IT services, and retail.

item.title: Kela's ₹177 Crore Bet: Contrarian Buys 4 Stocks Targeting Turnarounds
item.description: Veteran investor Madhusudan Kela invested ₹177 crore in four companies in the March 2026 quarter. His strategy targets contrarian bets and opportunities missed by most investors, including Aptech Ltd, Indiabulls Ltd, Simplex Infrastructures Ltd, and Subam Papers Ltd, all chosen for their turnaround or growth potential.

item.title: Mahindra Dominates India's Diesel SUVs as BS7 Costs Rise
item.description: Mahindra & Mahindra (M&M) now leads India's diesel passenger vehicle market, taking over half the share mainly thanks to its popular SUVs. But new BS7 emission standards are coming, which will make diesel cars much more expensive to build. This could change the diesel market significantly. M&M is already working on electric vehicles (EVs) and other engine types, as its strong position in diesel faces pressure from regulations and buyers wanting cleaner options.
